ground_truth.description: The class handles multiple responsibilities: order taking, complaints, discounts, customer contact, and deliveries
Using a primitive (`bool`) for `frequent_customer_discount` instead of a more expressive construct
Grouped customer details (`first_name`, `last_name`, `address`, `phone_number`, `email`) should be encapsulated
Method receives multiple unrelated parameters (`pizza_type`, `size`, `crust_type`, etc.)
Overuse of if-else statements to handle different complaints
Fields (`temp_discount_code`, `temp_order_note`) used only under specific conditions
Method is meant for inheritance but is empty
Multiple update methods modifying individual fields instead of a single encapsulated update
Splitting name updates across different methods instead of a single encapsulated update
Similar to `update_name`, updates should be encapsulated
Separate field update that contributes to shotgun surgery
Separate field update that contributes to shotgun surgery
Multiple notification methods each handling separate concerns
Related to `notify_for_promotion`, but managed separately
Another notification method that should be unified
Parallel implementation of discount and loyalty points logic
Another parallel logic similar to `apply_discount`
Method is present but never used in any flow
Unused method that can be removed
Another unused method
Repeated functionality with identical operations on the same attribute
Excessive reliance on another class (`chef`) for core functionality
Accessing internal methods of `chef` (`clean_kitchen()`)
Excessive method chaining (`self.chef.clean_kitchen()`)
Delegates work without adding value
Performs multiple actions in a single method, reducing readability
